inline_comments.message:
From the security perspective it's not recommended to store this password in a file as knowledge of this password allows you to generate all other passwords.

The recommended way is to set it via command line and additionally in more "production like" deployment add a " " before helm command (at least for Debian family distros) to prevent the command from being saved in bash history

So I can say, 'Add in the command line and put a " " before in order not to have it stored in history'?
Ar you OK with that (plus command line example update of course)

I'd prefer to modify this point to only mention that it should be set from cmd line in this point and then below add extra "Warning" box that master password is very sensitive informationa dn  if you are using this in production make sure that you prevent it from being stored in bash history. In debian-family systems this can be achieved by prepending a space before helm command

I believe that this way it would be more readable
